subtitle: Which government institutions are most dependent on a single vendor?

editorialLede.body: Institutional capture occurs when a government agency depends on a single vendor for the majority of its purchases. This phenomenon eliminates competition, inflates prices, and creates political dependencies that are difficult to break.
editorialLede.statLine: {{count}} institutions concentrate over 50% of their spending in a single vendor.

whyItMatters.label: WHY DOES IT MATTER?
whyItMatters.intro: Excessive dependence on a single vendor creates:
whyItMatters.prices: Inflated prices: without competition, the vendor sets the terms
whyItMatters.corruption: Corruption risk: the exclusive relationship facilitates favor-trading
whyItMatters.fragility: Operational fragility: if the vendor fails, the institution collapses
whyItMatters.opacity: Opacity: direct contracts without bidding are harder to audit

errorMessage: Could not load money flow data.
errorHint: Try reloading the page. If the problem persists, the service may be temporarily offline.

whatIsCapture.label: WHAT IS INSTITUTIONAL CAPTURE?
whatIsCapture.body: Institutional capture occurs when a single vendor wins such a large share of an institution's contracts that it effectively controls the institution's purchasing decisions. According to OECD guidelines, when one vendor accounts for more than 30% of an institution's total procurement spend, it is a significant red flag. Above 50%, it represents near-total dependence — a pattern associated with bid rigging, ghost contracts, and systemic corruption.

thresholds.total: Total Capture — one vendor controls >50% of spend
thresholds.high: High Risk — one vendor holds 30–50% of spend
thresholds.moderate: Moderate — one vendor holds 15–30% of spend

topCaptured.label: MOST CAPTURED INSTITUTIONS
topCaptured.sublabel: Ranked by the dominant vendor's share of total institutional spend

filters.noResults: No institutions meet the ≥{{pct}}% capture threshold for the current selection.

emptyState.title: No data available for this combination of filters.
emptyState.hint: Try a different sector or year, or reset filters to see all data.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.content: Calculated from 3.05M procurement contracts (2002–2025) with amounts above 100K MXN. The capture percentage is the vendor's share of the institution's total procurement spend across all contract types. High concentration (>30%) is a risk indicator per OECD procurement guidelines. Cell color reflects the intensity of capture — deeper red means greater dependence on a single vendor.
